Loomal lets you charge for what you sell online — API calls, tools, digital products, or your whole store. One line of code (or a Shopify/WooCommerce plugin) adds an agent-ready paywall: AI agents pay you in USDC, settled in about 2 seconds, and you keep 100% of your revenue — no percentage cut, ever. Free to start, no card; flat monthly plans as you grow. Every paid listing appears on the Loomal Index, where agents discover and pay. Launch offer: first 500 sellers get 1,000 transactions free.

### What does "Agent-Native Infrastructure" actually look like?

View all

Reviews

No reviews yetBe the first to leave a review for Loomal

Wispr Flow: Dictation That Works Everywhere

Wispr Flow: Dictation That Works EverywhereStop typing. Start speaking. 4x faster.

Promoted

Danny Heng

Loomal

Loomal

Maker

📌

👋 Hey Product Hunt!

I'm Danny, maker of Loomal.

🤖 AI agents are starting to buy things. They research, compare, and complete purchases on their own.

⚠️ But here's the problem. Almost nothing online can sell to them.

Stores and APIs are built for humans. Browsers. Credit cards. Checkout forms.

Agents don't have those. They have wallets. 👛

So we have this strange moment:

millions of agents with spending power, and an internet that can't take their money. 💸 [...] Subscribe

This is the 2nd launch from Loomal. View more [...] Report

Marcin Uchacz

good stuff! from the buyers' perspective: if my AI agent makes a mistake, how do I prove what it did and who authorized it? or there's no chance?

Report

Danny Heng

Loomal

Loomal

Maker

@marcin\_uchacz1 There's a chance — it's actually two layers.

On Loomal: every payment produces a signed receipt — which agent paid, which endpoint, amount, timestamp. So for transactions, the record exists by default.

"What did it do and who authorized it" beyond payments is an identity problem, and it's exactly why we built Mailgent (launched here last week): each agent gets its own cryptographic identity, actions are signed against it, and permissions are scoped — so you can show which agent did what, under whose authority, with proof.

Report

Rajesh

Loomal

Loomal

Maker

results.title: Loom Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
Based on 159 reviews

Review Loom?

Reviewers mostly see Loom as a simple, effective way to replace meetings with quick screen recordings, especially for demos, bug reports, onboarding, client updates, and remote teamwork. People repeatedly praise the fast recording-to-link workflow, ease of use, comments, transcripts, and the clearer tone video adds over email or long text. The main complaints are rising pricing, a widening gap in the free plan, basic editing, and reliability issues like laggy extensions, disconnects, upload slowdowns, stuttering playback, and weak video quality for some users.

Summarized with AI

Loom has been an absolute lifesaver for my teaching! The ease of use and the ability to record and share screen tutorials quickly have made it an indispensable tool in my virtual classroom. Thanks to Loom, I can engage my students effectively and provide them with valuable resources.

I find Loom helpful for quick demos shared with my team. I record tutorials at the start of new features. I personally trim recordings before distribution. Occasionally upload speeds slow during large captures. Still it saves me from writing lengthy instructions. I recommend it to creative teams.
